what_it_does: Implements a self-contained React design-canvas substrate: a pan/zoom viewport that hosts editable sections and fixed-size artboards, supports drag reorder, deletion, inline renaming, a fullscreen focus overlay, persisted layout state via a JSON sidecar, and PNG/HTML export of individual artboards.
observations: One-time injected <style> block with dc-prefixed classes provides all chrome styling.
State (section order, renamed titles/labels, hidden artboards) is read with fetch from .design-canvas.state.json and written via window.omelette.writeFile.
Viewport transform is stored in a ref and applied directly to the DOM for 60fps pan/zoom; the inverse scale is exposed as --dc-inv-zoom for zoom-invariant chrome.
Wheel events are classified as mouse-wheel zoom, trackpad pinch, or trackpad pan; Safari gesture events are handled separately.
Artboard drag-reorder uses pointer events and live translateX transforms on siblings, committing order only on drop.
dcExport clones the artboard DOM, inlines computed styles, fonts, images, and background images, then renders via SVG foreignObject to PNG at 3x or to a standalone HTML file.
Focus overlay is rendered through ReactDOM.createPortal to document.body and supports arrow-key navigation within and across sections.
DCArtboard is a marker component that returns null; DCArtboardFrame renders the actual card and chrome.
standouts: Full canvas interaction model (pan, zoom, drag-reorder, focus, export) in a single file with no external runtime dependencies.
Self-contained PNG/HTML export pipeline that bakes in fonts and images.
Zoom-invariant UI chrome using CSS custom property --dc-inv-zoom plus transform:scale and CSS zoom.
Persistence through a project-local JSON sidecar read by fetch and written through a host bridge.
conventions: dc- prefixed class and data-attribute names to avoid collisions with hosted design styles.
React context (DCCtx) for shared canvas state and mutations.
Inline styles over CSS classes for dynamic geometry and theme tokens.
Refs and direct DOM manipulation for high-frequency viewport updates.
Functional components with hooks; nested helper functions inside components/effects.
concerns: React and ReactDOM are used but no imports are shown in the excerpt; they must be provided by the host environment.
Global document/window access assumes a browser context and may fail in SSR or non-DOM tests.
CSS zoom property used for section-head counter-scaling is non-standard and may behave inconsistently across browsers.
Font CSS extraction relies on regex parsing and fetching cross-origin stylesheets, which can fail or miss fonts.
dcExport may fail for cross-origin images or large canvases, and uses XMLSerializer which can produce invalid XHTML for complex content.
Drag-reorder scale calculation assumes the slot's visual width is uniformly scaled by the viewport transform.
LocalStorage viewport key uses location.pathname, which could collide across deployments or sandbox origins.
The file is long and mixes viewport, state, export, and UI chrome concerns.
notes: A self-contained, browser-only React design canvas with pan/zoom, editable artboards, persisted layout, and built-in export.
questions: How are React and ReactDOM made available to this module?
What is the exact contract of the window.omelette host bridge?
Is DCPostIt fully implemented in the full file, or was it truncated in the excerpt?
How does the host handle __dc_present, __dc_zoom, __dc_set_zoom, and __dc_probe messages?
Are there constraints on artboard children to prevent overflow/scroll as the usage comment warns?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches implementation: it is a Figma-ish canvas wrapper with sections, artboards, post-its, reordering, editing, focus overlay, persistence, and no external dependencies.
apparent_themes: design-tool ergonomics in a lightweight substrate
persistence and state recovery across reloads
export fidelity by inlining external assets
intention_signals: It appears designed to give an omelette-hosted design preview a Figma-like surface for arranging, annotating, and exporting UI variants without pulling in a heavy design-tool library.
novelty: A single-file, dependency-free React canvas that combines viewport pan/zoom, persisted editable layout, drag reorder, focus mode, and a self-contained DOM-to-PNG/HTML export pipeline.
contribution: Provides the interactive canvas shell, persisted section/artboard ordering and labeling, zoom-invariant chrome scaling, focus overlay navigation, and artboard export capability that a fused system would retain.
summary_for_embedding: design-canvas.jsx is a self-contained React component that implements a pan/zoom design canvas with editable sections and artboards, drag reordering, persisted layout state, a fullscreen focus overlay, and PNG/HTML export of artboards.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to provide a Figma-like presentation and iteration surface for design variants inside an omelette-hosted project preview.

symbols.does: Recursively unwraps React.Fragment children into a flat array.
symbols.description: Walks React.Children and expands any Fragment nodes so that DCSection and DCArtboard children can be discovered by type even when grouped with <>...</>.

symbols.does: Stateful root component that owns canvas state and persistence.
symbols.description: Reads .design-canvas.state.json via fetch, builds registries of sections and artboards, provides a context API for mutations, and renders the pan/zoom viewport plus a focus overlay when active.

symbols.does: Document keydown handler that exits focus on Escape.
symbols.description: Attached by DesignCanvas to close the fullscreen focus overlay when the user presses Escape.

symbols.does: Document pointerdown handler that blurs active contentEditable elements.
symbols.description: Commits any in-progress inline rename when the user clicks outside the editable element.

symbols.does: Implements pan/zoom transform and input handling.
symbols.description: Manages a transform ref, applies it directly to the world DOM node, handles wheel/gesture/pointer events for zoom and pan, persists the transform to localStorage, and posts zoom updates to the host frame.

symbols.does: Immediately saves the current viewport transform to localStorage.
symbols.description: Used inside DCViewport's layout effect to ensure the latest pan/zoom is persisted on pagehide or unmount, bypassing the debounced save timer.

symbols.does: Zooms the viewport around a screen point and compensates for layout drift.
symbols.description: Computes the next scale, pins the world point under the cursor, applies the transform, and corrects vertical drift caused by --dc-inv-zoom reflows using a DOM marker.

symbols.does: Heuristic that distinguishes notched mouse wheels from trackpad scroll.
symbols.description: Returns true for line-mode deltas or large integer Y-only deltas, routing those events to fixed-step zoom rather than pan.

symbols.does: Routes wheel events to zoom or pan behavior.
symbols.description: Prevents default, skips concurrent Safari gestures, and dispatches between trackpad pinch zoom, mouse-wheel step zoom, and trackpad two-finger pan.

symbols.does: Begins a Safari trackpad pinch gesture.
symbols.description: Sets the gesturing flag, records the current scale as a base, and prevents default to keep the page from scaling.

symbols.does: Updates scale during a Safari pinch gesture.
symbols.description: Zooms around the gesture's client point using the cumulative scale relative to the gesture start base.

symbols.does: Ends a Safari pinch gesture.
symbols.description: Clears the gesturing flag and prevents default.

symbols.does: Starts a drag-pan on middle or background primary click.
symbols.description: Captures the pointer and initializes drag state when the user middle-clicks anywhere or left-clicks on the canvas background.

symbols.does: Updates viewport translation during drag-pan.
symbols.description: Applies pointer deltas to the transform ref and re-applies the transform for live panning.

symbols.does: Ends drag-pan and releases pointer capture.
symbols.description: Restores the default cursor and clears drag state.

symbols.does: Handles postMessage commands from the host frame.
symbols.description: Applies host-driven zoom around the viewport center and re-announces canvas presence in response to __dc_probe messages.

symbols.does: Renders an editable section head and a row of ordered artboards.
symbols.description: Reads persisted order and hidden state from context, computes a stable artboard order, and renders DCArtboardFrame instances with rename/reorder/delete/focus callbacks.

symbols.does: Marker component for artboard children.
symbols.description: Returns null; its props are read by DesignCanvas and DCSection to build registries, while DCArtboardFrame performs the actual rendering.

symbols.does: Exports an artboard DOM node to PNG or standalone HTML.
symbols.description: Waits for fonts, collects and inlines @font-face rules, recursively clones the node with computed styles, inlines images and background images, then either serializes to an SVG foreignObject for PNG rasterization at 3x or wraps the clone in a minimal HTML document.

symbols.does: Fetches a URL and returns it as a data URL.
symbols.description: Used by dcExport to inline external images, fonts, and background-image URLs as base64 or data URIs.

symbols.does: Fetches a stylesheet and extracts @font-face blocks.
symbols.description: Recursively handles @import URLs and regex-extracts @font-face rules from cross-origin stylesheets that cannot be read via cssRules.

symbols.does: Recursively walks CSS rule lists to collect @font-face rules.
symbols.description: Traverses style sheets, import rules, and nested rule groups, recording font-face CSS text with a base URL for later URL resolution.

symbols.does: Builds a single CSS string of inlined @font-face rules.
symbols.description: Awaited expression inside dcExport that resolves relative font URLs to absolute data URLs and concatenates the rules for embedding in the exported clone.

symbols.does: Recursively clones a DOM node with computed styles inlined.
symbols.description: Copies the source tree, strips scripts and comments, converts canvas elements to images, and writes the element's computed CSS into inline style attributes while disabling animations and transitions.

symbols.does: Triggers a browser download of a Blob.
symbols.description: Creates a temporary anchor element with an object URL, sets the download filename, clicks it, and revokes the URL after a short delay.

symbols.does: Renders the chrome and card for one artboard.
symbols.description: Displays the header with grip, editable label, menu, and focus button; hosts drag-reorder logic; and renders the artboard's children inside a sized card.

symbols.does: Closes the artboard menu on outside pointerdown.
symbols.description: Effect handler that dismisses the kebab menu when the user clicks outside it and resets the delete confirmation state.

symbols.does: Initiates PNG or HTML export for the artboard.
symbols.description: Closes the menu, derives a safe filename from the label, and calls dcExport on the card DOM node.

symbols.does: Begins a live drag-reorder of an artboard.
symbols.description: Captures pointer events, computes the current viewport scale, records sibling slot positions, and moves the dragged card while translating peers into their new slots in real time.

symbols.does: Applies translateX offsets to peers during drag-reorder.
symbols.description: Updates sibling slot transforms so they visually slide into the positions implied by the live order without changing DOM order until drop.

symbols.does: Updates dragged card position and live order during reorder.
symbols.description: Translates the dragged slot by the screen-space delta divided by viewport scale, then recomputes the nearest slot and re-layouts peers.

symbols.does: Commits or cancels drag-reorder on pointer release.
symbols.description: Removes listeners, snaps the dragged card to its final slot with a transition, clears transforms, and calls onReorder if the order changed.

symbols.does: Renders a contentEditable inline text field.
symbols.description: Commits changes on blur or Enter, stops pointer propagation so clicks don't start canvas pan, and forwards the new text to onChange.

symbols.does: Renders a fullscreen overlay for one artboard with navigation.
symbols.description: Portals a fixed-position overlay to document.body, scales the selected artboard to fit the screen, shows section dropdown and index, and handles arrow-key navigation within and across sections.

symbols.does: Navigates to the previous or next artboard in the same section.
symbols.description: Wraps around the section's slot list and calls setFocus with the new slot identifier.

symbols.does: Navigates to the first artboard of an adjacent non-empty section.
symbols.description: Steps past sections with no visible artboards and focuses the first slot of the next or previous section in sectionOrder.

symbols.does: Keyboard handler for focus overlay navigation.
symbols.description: Maps ArrowLeft/ArrowRight to go and ArrowUp/ArrowDown to goSection, preventing default scrolling.

symbols.does: Renders a side arrow button for focus overlay navigation.
symbols.description: Local component inside DCFocusOverlay that shows a circular button on the left or right edge and calls the provided onClick handler.

symbols.does: Renders a positioned sticky-note-like annotation.
symbols.description: Component signature accepts children, top/left/right/bottom, rotation, and width; the full implementation is truncated in the provided excerpt but it is intended as a canvas annotation element.
